Tutorial:
Battle:

The first aspect of the game that is taught in the tutorial is the battle system.
When going into battle, a blue area can be selected to adjust the lineup of shikigami that are going to fight. To change a shikigami drag one in the card line to the position of your choice. The yellow determines which shikigami can spectate, you can put up to 2 and its used for raising purposes.

All characters' action orders will be displayed on the action bar. Tap the skill you want to use to select it. Following that, select the target the skill is going to be released on. Skills can be used to attack, defend, heal, and so on depending on the shikigami or onmyouji.

The skills can cost onibi, they are the energy points required to release special skils. You start the battle with 3 and can have a maximum of 8. It replenishes every 5 of your turns.

The view is able to be changed by dragging the battlefield. Also, the speed of animations can be adjusted between 1x or 2x speed.
 

At the battle's conclusion, different screens will show depending on the outcome. If victory is achieved, the experience gain and drops will be displayed, while a loss nets nothing. Screens are progressed through via tapping.
 

Summon:

Most shikigami can be summoned. Onmyouji can enter into a contract with youkai, upon which they are called shikigami. There are several ways to summon shikigami. One of the most basic methods to summon is to draw charms on amulets. Shikigami can also be summoned by contract scroll in the book of shikigami once enough shards have been collected.

You start with Sanbi no Kitsune, Yuki Onna, and a random N shikigami. They are fixed and cannot be changed.
- To know more about summon click here. To check all available shikigami click here.

Level:

All shikigami can be leveled to become a six-star shikigami. They can also be awakened which boosts base stats, unlocks or strengthens skills.

A shikigami is summoned at level 1 and can go up to level 40. Shikigami usually are summoned with 2 stars, but there are rare cases that they can come with 3 stars or more.

To raise their levels maximize the current level and feed them cards with the ammount of the stars needed. Example: to raise a shikigami to a 3 star, you need 2 shikigami that are 2 star. To 4 you need 3 3 stars and so on.

 * Exception are blue darumas whose stars cannot be raised and thus cannot reach level 40. Black daruma are unable to reach 6 star.
- To learn more about nurturing shikigami visit Shikigami Records

Mitama:

Soul are products of wandering Souls. They can be equipped to make the shikigami stronger by boosting their various stats and giving special effects, and once dropped are found in the Shikigami Book.

Soul equipment slots have six positions. Equipping the same Soul in different slots will activate set effects. Soul can be strengthened for better stats, and strengthening requires other Soul and gold as strengthening materials. They can reach a maximum of level 15, starting from level 0.

- To know more about Mitama click here. To check all available Mitama click here.

Exploration:

When a chapter of the story is complete, a new area is opened on the map. Special missions called exploration instances can be challenged in these areas. The instances provide gold and experience. Some lesser youkai have increased rewards. each battle in the story maps costs 3 sushi stamina.

In story related content, the player taps on a target to begin the battle. When battling, there is chance for a boss to appear, winning against the boss activates spirit sight.

Additionally, other gameplay mechanisms are found on the Exploration map, such as the Soul dungeons, which drop Soul, and the Awakening dungeons which drop awakening materials.

Hyakki Yakou:

Available through the Village, the Hyakki Yakou is a way to gain shikigami shards.

The Hyakki Yakou can be entered with a friend to improve chances, and once a shikigami is selected from three random ones the Hyakki Yakou starts. If one is captured in the event then extra contract book shards are given. Capturing a shikigami in the procession involves tossing fortune beans at it.

- To know more about the Hyakki Yakou click here.

Learning Missions:

Completing 4 learning missions gives 10K gold and 30 beads, 8 gives a mysterious amulet and 30 beads, 13 gives a mysterious amulet and 40 beads.

Soul/Strengthening:
Soul can be strengthened to achieve better attributes.

Strengthening:

The "+" indicator on the Soul represents its level. Soul begin at level 0 and can be strengthened to level 15. Soul must be strengthened by sacrificing other Soul (where that Soul's experience is partially inherited) and they cannot level past the level cap, nor can they be ranked up to increase star rating. Strengthening also requires gold. The cost to strengthening depends on the stars of the Soul.

For secondary attribute chances when strengthening Soul to levels 3/6/9/12/15:
- If Soul has 4 secondary attributes, an existing secondary attribute will be randomly raised. Every attribute has 1/4 chance of being raised. Otherwise, existing substats have 9% chance, and new substat chance is 100%-9%*number of substats.
- If Soul's secondary attribute does not reach 4, then a sub-attribute is randomly added. Add chances are: attack-type 36%, defense-type 36%, utility-type 28%. (Includes attributes that already exist, it is possible to not get a new one)

Consignment Shop (寄售屋/委託販売屋):

The Consignment Shop allows players to trade S-Jade & special tickets for certain Skins, Skin Shards, Shards, & Accessories.

These tickets are Consignment Tickets & Jade Tickets. Consignment Tickets are used to purchase shards and accessories and maybe be purchased with S-Jade & Jade. Jade Tickets are received from doing Consignment Shop sales and can be used to exchange for Gold/Coins & Jade.

Wanted Quests:
Bounty is a mechanism located in the courtyard, which is unlocked at player character level 12. It is accessed via the "Seal" button underneath the Sakura tree.

Introduction:

Bounty is a method for players to gain Shikigami shards (including SSR), gold, jades, as rewards from completing missions.

Regardless of the progress of completion, the Bounty refreshes daily at 5:00 and 18:00 sharp, with a counter showing the next time it refreshes. Each refresh replaces the old bounty targets with 3 new ones. Players have a limit of completing 5 missions between each refresh: 3 of their own, and 2 accepted from friends.

Mission progress can be seen without returning to the bounty interface as defeating a target spirit pops up a scroll showing the progress.

Missions Types:

Common Quests:

The default missions clearly state the target and chapter locations. They are completed solo. They are accompanied by flavour text.

Special Quests:

There is a small chance for special missions to be given among the 3 missions refreshed.

Note that missions that allow inviting friends are limited to ones that are currently online, and some also have level limitations. The invite friends pop-up will automatically filter them in these cases. A successful invitation shows up as a message in the friend chat as well as the friend's own wanted quests.
- For more information visit the Location List page.

Types:

 

- For more information visit the Hint List page.
Can be identified via a lack of a name and a visual.

Location of the spirits are not given, and their identity must be deduced through descriptions and hints or via the wanted quest clues section in the ingame Help.

The mission has two progress bars, one for each player, and a button to add the second player.

The mission rewards are like normal, but a friend must be invited to complete it together. Should a friend not complete a co-op quest, there is the option to remove them after 2 hours from the invite and keep the mission progress for the sake of the next helper.

These missions are clearly distinguished with a different appearance. There is a blue ring encircling them much like other AR mechanisms, and a purple button to invite a friend, which is optional. After being tapped on, the player is transported to the real world. In the real world, players must use the in-game camera to search their surroundings for the spirit that must be defeated.

- For more information visit the Spirit Clue List page.
These missions are clearly distinguished with a different appearance. There is a blue ring encircling them much like other AR mechanisms, and a purple button to invite a friend, which is optional. After being tapped on, the in-game camera is opened to take a picture of the clue related to the spirit with the button on the right. If the image identification is successful, the mission is completed.
 

Food Quests:

When the pet backyard system becomes available, an additional 4th wanted quest will appear every time instead of 3. The extra quest is guaranteed to be a Food Quest. Completing a Food quest is the same as completing common quests and friend co-op quests.

Rules:

- KO's are only counted if the battle is won.
- Winning against a shikigami during friendly sparring does not count. (Does count for GL Server, except for zombie dog..
- Instead, the instances where the KO's do matter are: Various instances like Exploration, Kekkai Toppa, Soul, Youki Fuuin, Shikigami Challenge, Arena, Inkai Gate, Oumagatoki.
- Each mission has a star rating, indicating the level of the rewards.

Rewards:

Rewards are clearly indicated for each mission. Usually, the higher the star rating the better the rewards. If rewards are not manually retrieved after completing a mission, they will be sent to the mail on refresh.

Courtyard Shikigami:

Kohaku can be switched out for whatever shikigami whose bios were fully unlocked in whichever skin. Note that position of button changes based on shikigami height (even though they sit down). This is possible even when shikigami is no longer in the records (as long as it's in collection it's fine).

Due to different courtyard skins, the poses/perspectives of the shikigami will also change.

Exploration:
Exploring the map allows the player to fight in the various zones to obtain materials. During events, the map may undergo changes to reflect the story.

Explore Map:

 
Exploremap2.jpg
Exploremap3.jpg
 

Pact Shards:

- Multiple zones can be accessed this area. Such as:
- Shikigami Shards: Shard zones found after clearing a Challenge will appear here. Includes shard zones found by friends.
- EXP Spirit: A zone that yields a large amount of EXP upon being defeated.
- Coin Spirit: A zone that yields a large amount of coins upon being defeated.
- Mentorship Trial: A zone that can only be accessed by mentors and their apprentices.
- The EXP and Coin Spirit can be cleared with 1 host and 1-4 guests. Unlike the pop-up team zones, guests are not need to challenge the zones.

Team Zones:

- After clearing an explore zone, the is chance to find pop-up team zones. Such as: 
- Demon Seal
- Kraken (Mon-Fri) and Enraged Kraken (Sat-Sun)
- Nen
- After clearing stage 10 of the Orochi Soul Zone, the is chance to find Ultimate Orochi.
- These zones require 1 host and 1-2 guests. A minimum of 1 guest is need to challenge the zones.
- Additional zones may be available during events.

Right Sidebar:

- It has shortcuts to explore zones and displays unlocked Challenge Zones.
- Challenge Zones can only be unlocked in chapters 1-18.

Bottom Bar:

- It shows shortcuts to various zones. More information about the zones can be found on their individual pages. 
- Evo Zones
- Soul Zones
- Realm Raid
- Totem
- Heian Tales
- Shikigami Dispatch
- Secret Zones
- Area Boss
- Heian Story
- Six Realms Gates

Explore Zones:

Explore:

- Challenge the shikigami minions in the zone and win to earn rewards.
- There are different rewards for different minions: 
- Coin Bonus: Increases the coins earned by x 2.
- EXP Bonus: Increases the EXP earned by x 2.
- Drop Bonus: Increases the drop chances for all rewards.
- Each time a minion is defeated, there is a chance of the zone boss to appearing.
- When the zone boss is defeated, paper doll may look for treasure for you to receive.
- Harder versions of explore zones become unlocked after reaching certain player levels.

Boss Challenge:

- When paper doll looks for treasure, there is a chance for them to find a spirit leader.
- Once a spirit leader has been found, a Boss Challenge Pass can be used to challenge it.
- If you clear a Boss Challenge zone, you can get shards of the spirit leader and have a chance to find a Shikigami Shard zone. 
- The Shikigami Shard zone is accessed via the Pact Shards zone.
- Level for the enemies in challenge zones increase each time the zone has been cleared. Max is level 40.

Introduction:

Friends (好友/友達) is the third option in the scroll menu from right-to-left on the main page. There you can add friends, send texts, exchange friend points, see your homepage and a lot of other features that will be explained in this article.

Friends Pane:

Lists show the name of your friend, picture, level, along with when they were last seen online in the game and the send and receive Friendship icon. The icon can also show a flower instead of a heart, meaning they are your best friend.
 

Current Server:

You can have up to 80 friends.

The friend point icon also has different colors. Yellow means you received friend points, orange sent friend points and gray received and sent friend points.

Cross-server:

- The cross-server list is only available for Chinese servers.
Can add friends so they can be invited for teams, but cannot send any points to them and vice versa.

Blacklist:

Players can be added to the blacklist so that they cannot bother you any more.

Can create a group chat. Automatically disbands if noone comes online in a certain number of days.

Can join 20 groups max and each group can have up to 30 people.

Shows the 10 recent contacts (not necessarily friends) with players, and the 20 recent players that were teamed with.
 

Options:

- For general information on what happens when tapping on a player, see Avatar
On the right side you will see a toggle with two arrows, by tapping on it will show the following features:
- Page: leads to your friend's homepage.
- Spar: allows you to spar with your friend if they are online as well.
- Group: invites friend to a group. Can invite 10 people at once with the dialog, whether they are on or offline.
- Kekkai: directs you to your friend's Kekkai.
- Push: Needs to enable app setting to give push notification. Will notify player if friend comes online.
- Bond: shows the stages for the best friend function.
- Remove: deletes them from record (is not deletion of friend).

Gifts:

Sending friend fortune bags to each other increases friend bond points. Once purchased, the system automatically sends it to your friendand they will receive a notification.

- Friendship Pack
- Limit of buying 2 and receiving 1 a day (refreshes at midnight). This mechanism may also change in times of events.
- Costs: 30 beads
- Condition: Buy the gift and invite a friend, 15 growth points are needed to get the reward. (Each time the buyer teams with the invited friend to clear Soul, awakening, exploration, Tako, Nian, Youki Fuuin, Experience Youkai, or Gold Youkai, 1 growth point is given.)
- Rewards: Orochi reverse scale, 2-star Heavenly Thunder Drum kekkai card, Oitsukigami shard and gold.
- Chance to get: Mysterious amulet, black daruma shard, white daruma, blue daruma.
- Friend Gift Bag
- Limit of buying 10 and receiving 10 a day (refreshes at midnight).
- Costs: 6 tamashiidama
- Rewards for Friend: 1 friend fortune bag (random reward of either a blue daruma, a black daruma, gold, stamina, or current world amulet), with chance to turn into a great fortune bag.
- Rewards for Self: 30 beads

Realm Trial:
This was the prelude event to Journey Home, where Suzuka Gozen asked for Ukiyo Aoandon's help to review the events of Sealand's campaign against Heian-Kyo to be able to understand what happened to Ootakemaru and Mt. Suzuka in her long absence. The event focused on Suzuka Gozen and Ukiyo Aoandon following Ootakemaru throughout the war within Aoandon's realm. It also served as a quick recap for the Sealand saga for the players who missed past events.

During the boss phase of this 6-day event players weren't allowed to leave their guilds, as ranking required contribution from top 40 guild members and was strictly guild versus guild. The event didn't have a shop and the rewards were obtained from quests and guild rankings. Notable rewards for this event were the still and animated versions of the Whale Bone icon frame, and the Sealand's Conquest guild frame.

The scroll interface can be seen in the Courtyard or Village, once a mechanism is entered into, the scroll may disappear. When the scroll is rolled up, the gold, beads, and stamina counters also collapse.

- Town: Goes to the Town. Depending on the graphic quality selected for the Town,

The lanterns on the wishing rack act as wrappers for various Onmyoji related websites

- Event: Serves as the entrance for the current major event happening in-game
- Navigation: Links to the game helper which answers many questions.
- Events: Links to the events page, showing various holidays, online and offline events, and other pertinent happenings.
- Survey: Occasionally serves a survey for players to complete to gather feedback for the future progress of the game.
- Fanworks: Links to the official website where players can upload their fanworks, and others can browse through them.
- Customer Service: Connects to a chat with a customer support representative, along with a FAQ, to help solve problems.

China:

- Redeem Code: Opens a window where a PIN can be entered to redeem for in-game items.

Japan:

- Bulletin Board: Onmyoji forums
- Official Wiki: The official Japanese wiki, can also be accessed at https://kamigame.jp/陰陽師/index.html.

Mechanisms:

 

The onmyoji can be tapped on to switch to them or change their skin. The same can be done with the active player onmyoji, but there is an additional option to change them to any shikigami with their 3 main bios unlocked.

By tapping the tree, main story chapters and old memory scrolls can be viewed or the courtyard skin can be changed.

Paper dolls will dispense buffs, offer shortcuts to active events, and provide other daily rewards. They may also serve as the redemption mechanism for some event rewards. Appearance may change depending on events or equipped skins.

The shikigami that offers bounty can be changed if all their bios can be unlocked. Each shikigami has a different pose.

- For more information visit Courtyard Pet.
Courtyard pets will provide special rewards after clear an Orochi soul zones when they are well fed and happy.

Other things may show up in the courtyard due to events.

Soul:
Souls (御魂) are the different equipment you can apply to your Shikigami to increase their stats and obtain unique effects. A player can keep 6000 unequipped souls.

Introduction:

Type:

Souls are often grouped into different categories based on the different effects bequeathed. A master list can be found here.

Officially, the Soul's attributes are considered attack-type, defense-type, and utility-type.
 

colspan="4" style="text-align: center; background:#a51919;"| Attack-type

ATK 攻击 / 攻撃力
ATK Bonus / ATK% 攻击加成 / 追加攻撃力
Crit 暴击 / 会心率
Crit DMG 暴击伤害 / 会心DMG

colspan="4" style="text-align: center; background:#a51919;" | Defense-type

HP 生命 / HP
HP Bonus / HP% 生命加成 / 追加HP
DEF 防御 / 防御力
DEF Bonus / DEF% 防御加成 / 追加防御力

colspan="4" style="text-align: center; background:#a51919;" | Utility-type

Effect RES 效果抵抗 / 効果抵抗
Effect HIT 效果命中 / 効果命中
SPD 速度 / 素早さ
 Special attributes are considered to be: Speed, Effect Resist, Effect Hit, Crit, Crit Damage Other attributes are: Attack, Health, Defense, Attack Bonus, Health Bonus, Defense Bonus.

Stars:

The higher the star rating of a Soul, the stronger it is compared to the same Soul of lesser star rating, as levelling a higher-star Soul causes a higher rate of attribute enhancement. Souls have one star at minimum and six stars at maximum.

However, the attribute enhancement is not necessarily fixed as it seems that decimals are added at random, thus, only the max attributes are certain.

- To see the table with stats data click here.

Positions:

Like any other game with an equipment system, every shikigami in this game has six different slots to equip Souls. However, what makes Onmyoji's implementation slightly confusing is the fact that the Soul enhances a specific stat in a specific way depending on its position, and the main stat is unrelated to the effect of the Soul itself. Specifically, consult the following to see what stats are enhanced for each of the six positions:
 
 

colspan="2" style="text-align: center; background:#a51919;" | Main stats for each slot

style="background:#a51919;"| Slot 1
ATK

style="background:#a51919;"| Slot 2
SPD, ATK Bonus, DEF Bonus, HP Bonus

style="background:#a51919;"| Slot 3
DEF

style="background:#a51919;"| Slot 4
Effect HIT, Effect RES, ATK Bonus, DEF Bonus, HP Bonus

style="background:#a51919;"| Slot 5
HP

style="background:#a51919;"| Slot 6
Crit, Crit DMG, ATK Bonus, DEF Bonus, HP Bonus

 Thus, while all Souls can have different sub stats, slots 2, 4, and 6 (directly left, lower right, and upper right) can have different main stats.

Stats:

- For more information, visit Mitama/Strengthening

Main Stat:

The text that is in red when accessing the information panel of the Soul is its main stat. The main star is guaranteed to increase as the Soul levels up. The possible main stats of the Soul depend on its position.

Sub Stats:

The text that is in black when accessing the information panel of the Soul shows its sub stat, which is upgraded or added randomly at levels 3, 6, 9, 12, and 15. There are a maximum of 4 sub stats, which can be of any type and is unrelated to the Soul's position.

Combo Attribute:

The text that is greyed out when accessing the information panel of the Soul is the 2-set and 4-set combo effect. When 2 or 4 of the same type of Soul is equipped on one shikigami, the corresponding effect activates and the text turns green. These attributes are specific to the Soul type and cannot be powered up through levelling.

Attribute Additions:

Altar:

The altar mechanism is a method to recycle large amounts of unwanted Soul for gold. Up to 100 Soul can be offered to the altar at one time, but there is no upper limit to the total number of Soul that can be offered.

In addition to gold, there is a chance to get a divine reward if Soul that are 4 star or higher are offered. Rewards include beads, awakening materials, shikigami shards (including SSR) and kekkai cards. Furthermore, each time any Soul is sold, there is a probability for "bestseller" effect, which gives bonus gold.

Management:

Gluttonous Ghost:

 入口：式神录→御魂→整理→贪吃鬼
 用处：贪吃鬼会定期吃掉标记为弃置的御魂并存储等量的御魂经验。
 阴阳师大人可通过「进食习惯」设置哪些星级的御魂在获得的时候自动弃置，以便贪吃鬼进食。也可通过「进食习惯」设置贪吃鬼吃掉弃置御魂的进食周期（默认为停止进食）。在大人们登录游戏的时候，贪吃鬼会吃掉弃置时间超过所设定周期的御魂。
 注意事项：贪吃鬼不会吃掉青吉鬼、6星御魂、强化等级≥1的御魂，并且贪吃鬼也不能帮大人自动弃置6星御魂。使用贪吃鬼强化其他御魂时，只会消耗其存储的经验，不会使其摧毁。贪吃鬼不可用于强化御灵。
 储存上限：贪吃鬼可存储经验上限是99999999，超过就不会再进食了。
Players can find the gluttonous ghost function under the “Souls” tab. Gluttonous Ghost will eat souls that are marked as discarded and store the equivalent amount as soul experience at fixed intervals. 

Players can change the settings of which souls to discard automatically with the “eating habits” function, to facilitate easier use of the gluttonous ghost feature. “Eating Habits” can also be used to change the time interval at which the gluttonous ghost eats souls. The default setting is “never.” When the player logs into the game, the gluttonous ghost will eat the discarded souls that has been discarded for a period of time which exceeds the set time interval. 

The gluttonous ghost will not eat Aokichi Oni, g6 souls, or souls that have been upgraded to level 1 or higher, and will not automatically discard g6 souls. The upper limit of experience stored by the gluttonous ghost is 99999999, and it will not eat souls in excess of that. 

Gluttonous Ghost cannot be used to strengthen Goryou. 

Acquiring:

Souls can be acquired by several different methods.

While doing story quests and subsequent exploration of those same areas, you will often find one or two after beating an enemy. Each chapter location rewards a single type of Soul. Soul up to four stars are dropped from this method.

- Orochi and Sougenbi at the Soul Zone
- A harder version of Orochi, the True Yamata no Orochi, will also appear at random at the southwest of the Soul Zone.
- In the Goryou Realm, which are opened on various days of week, all levels of Goryou instances will drop random Souls.
- Oumagatoki, Hunt, Tako, Inkai Gate will also have give Souls.
- One-time completion of certain Skin Instance floors may give Souls.
- Certain delegations will give a specified Soul.
- The Shop

Other uses:

- Goryou must be fed Soul in order to level up

Trivia:

- Souls are also sentient

Fiery Chime:
Fiery Chime was the release event of the SSR shikigami Suzuhiko Hime. Having travelled to the perilous Snow Realm, Seimei and friends along with Jinten Tamamo no Mae and Enmusubi find themselves trapped in a host of memories. Gradually they uncover the secret past of the amnesia-stricken Madame Saint of the Frostfolk, and the motives of her High Priest.

Players received Affinity Bells daily to challenge Peak Altar, the event's main farming mode, in which players would break through covered tiles to earn the event currency Pine Pendants and find the entrance to the next stage. Completing daily quests and accumulating Illuminating Chimes would unlock event rewards in Illuminated Steps.

Heavenly Encounters housed a series of mini-games such as Tesso's wheel of fortune, Jikikaeru's staged fights and various boss fights, and a shareable Merchant's shop. The Mountain Tales side stories were also unlocked here daily. 

Notable rewards included Orochi's Shivering Shadow skin, the animated frame Chiming Shrine, and three exclusive illustrations. The exclusive Fiery Chime theme and touch interface effect, as well as the Peak Altar realm skin, could be unlocked in Illuminated Steps.

Chimes in the Snow:

Short story fragments unlocked after every five stages in Peak Altar.

 

Travelers… The snowfield may look calm and peaceful, but unpredictable dangers lurk beneath the snow. If you don’t tread carefully, you will fall into the cracks. Only journey into the storm if you’re familiar with the path.

I’ve been living in Mt. Skywatch for decades and I’ve witnessed travelers of all walks of life. They relax and enjoy a beverage at my place to regain their energy for their journey. Speaking of which, even Madame Saint likes my liquor. Would you like to try some?

The young generation of Frostfolk are born and raised in the freezing wind of the snowfield. They do not fear the cold. They embrace it. Although we live in the highland of the Snow Realm, we live a quiet life with no interaction with the outside world. We have a Saint born from the Holy Fire on the peak of Mt. Skywatch. She brings a different kind of warmth to the bright white snowfield…

I hate this white snowy land. I always find it lonely and borderless and it always makes me feel empty inside. But ever since I touched the sacred flame, the heat stayed inside my heart. Madame Saint of Mt. Skywatch is the undying flame of us Frostfolk.

The Frostfolk are good at singing. After all, the vast snowfield is a natural stage. Hahaha, no kidding. But I prefer the dancing of Madame Saint to the beautiful voices of our clan. It’s said Madame Saint learned the Rite Dance from the High Priest… I wonder what the High Priest’s version would be like. 

My butcher’s shop is the most popular one in Mt. Skywatch. You wanna know why? Hehe, that’s because Madame Saint visited my butcher’s while she was on a fieldtrip down the mountain. She even set one of my chopping boards on fire. Not everyone can have Madame Saint’s blessing. I still have that scorched chopping board at home.

We’re good at making use of everything that nature can offer. See those towering statues? They're sculpted with ice. The most glorious and most beautiful one looks like our Madame Saint. She has blessed us so we should be humbled.

Us Frostfolk never believed in temples. After all, we have Madame Saint on Mt. Skywatch. If we wanted to pray, we’d go directly to the Shrine.

Why are you still wearing such thin clothes, outlander? Come on in and warm yourself by the fire. The snowstorm is coming. If you don’t come in soon, you’ll be buried in the snow. I saw a young man with bow and arrows and a fox spirit a few days ago. I wonder where they went…

I climbed up Mt. Skywatch once and saw the legendary Madame Saint. Mother was right about her. She’s truly divine and beautiful, emanating fiery heat that could light up the snowfield. Madame Saint will hear my prayers and bless us. But, Madame Saint, why did Mother leave me…
